title: Time word problems with number line
description: Use a number line to help solve elapsed time word problems.
topic_path: Time word problems with number line

assessment_items.question_content:
It takes Maxime $27$ minutes to walk to school. She starts walking to school at $8{:}15\text{ a.m.}$

**Which number line below graphs when Maxime arrives at school?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $8{:}15$ when Maxime leaves for school.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each tick mark on the number line represents $1$ minute.
So, we need to move **right** on the number line $27$ minutes.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{8{:}15} +{} &\greenD{20}\text{ minutes} = 8{:}35\\\\
8{:}35 +{} & \purpleD{5}\text{ minutes} = 8{:}40 \\\\
8{:}40 +{}&\goldE{2}\text{ minutes} = \goldE{8{:}42}\\\\
\end{align}$
$\greenD{20}\text{ minutes}+\purpleD{5}\text{ minutes}+\goldE{2}\text{ minutes}=27\text{ minutes}$
Maxime arrives at school at $8{:}42\text{ a.m.}$

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A number line labeled 8:15 to 9:00 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point appears at the 8:42 tick mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/39b8c1d65d77b3a2d661e68ed1abda261ff0909c)
![A number line labeled 8:15 to 9:00 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point appears at the 8:37 tick mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dcb48c5af192f24e2b2dc9c7c9c5dfd3ccdacf8f)
![A number line labeled 8:15 to 9:00 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point appears at the 8:47 tick mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3f2fbbf971263a38d595c5d6bf2ac6e044823510)

assessment_items.question_content:
Bernardo starts playing his trumpet at $5{:}11\text{ p.m.}$ He finishes $33$ minutes later. 

**Which number line below graphs when Bernardo finishes playing his trumpet?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $5{:}11$ when Bernardo starts playing his trumpet.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each tick mark on the number line represents $1$ minute.
So, we need to move **right** on the number line $33$ minutes.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{5{:}11} +{} &\greenD{4}\text{ minutes} = 5{:}15\\\\
5{:}15 +{} & \purpleD{25}\text{ minutes} = 5{:}40 \\\\
5{:}40 +{}&\goldE{4}\text{ minutes} = \goldE{5{:}44}\\\\
\end{align}$
$\greenD{4}\text{ minutes}+\purpleD{25}\text{ minutes}+\goldE{4}\text{ minutes}=33\text{ minutes}$
Bernardo finishes playing his trumpet at $5{:}44\text{ p.m.}$

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A number line labeled 5:00 to 5:45 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point is marked at 5:44.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e29f289dbf95100888b64c6b80604eff8a647831)
![A number line labeled 5:00 to 5:45 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point is marked at 5:11.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e95b9c6d9541e30c2cb2525c72c6a93bcea2a58c)
![A number line labeled 5:00 to 5:45 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point is marked at 5:38.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cc34af284542dc50ff09146cdb63986f76917eff)

assessment_items.question_content:
Yasmin was at swim practice for $19$ minutes. She starts practice at $8{:}23\text{ a.m.}$ 

**Which number line below graphs when Yasmin finishes swim practice?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $8{:}23$ when Yasmin starts swim practice.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each tick mark on the number line represents $1$ minute.
So, we need to move **right** on the number line $19$ minutes.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{2}\text{ minutes}+\purpleD{15}\text{ minutes}+\goldE{2}\text{ minutes}=19\text{ minutes}$
Yasmin finishes swim practice at $8{:}42\text{ a.m.}$

[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Mohamed speaks on the phone for $15$ minutes. He ends his phone call at  $9{:}20\text{ a.m.}$

**Which number line below graphs when Mohamed starts his phone call?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $9{:}20$ when Mohamed finishes his phone call. 

[[☃ image 1]]

Each tick mark on the number line represents $1$ minute.
So, we need to move **left** on the number line $15$ minutes.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldE{5}\text{ minutes}+\purpleD{5}\text{ minutes}+\greenD{5}\text{ minutes}=15\text{ minutes}$
Mohamed starts his phone call at $9{:}05\text{ a.m.}$

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A number line labeled 9:00 to 9:45 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point is marked at 9:10.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f2ec11809accb24d865302665b546d56f5ad486d)

assessment_items.question_content:
Rohit reads for $32$ minutes. He finishes reading at  $12{:}55\text{ p.m.}$ 

**Which number line below graphs when Rohit starts reading?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $12{:}55$ when Rohit finishes reading. 

[[☃ image 1]]
Each tick mark on the number line represents $1$ minute.

So, we need to move **left** on the number line $32$ minutes.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{12{:}55} -{} &\greenD{10}\text{ minutes} = 12{:}45\\\\
12{:}45 -{} & \purpleD{20}\text{ minutes} = 12{:}25 \\\\
12{:}25 -{}&\goldE{2}\text{ minutes} = \goldE{12{:}23}\\\\
\end{align}$
$\greenD{10}\text{ minutes}+\purpleD{20}\text{ minutes}+\goldE{2}\text{ minutes}=32\text{ minutes}$

Rohit starts reading at $12{:}23\text{ p.m.}$

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A number line labeled 12:15 to 1:00 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point is marked at 12:17.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/37d29a677bf158b417531bd2b341be770b5dd28a)
![A number line labeled 12:15 to 1:00 with tick marks every 1 unit. A dot is marked at 12:32.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/df2ebae11ede1978e3d00c58e0f3042c9ea6807b)
![A number line labeled 12:15 to 1:00 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point is marked at 12:23.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b46667577d6987c4f6575a02bea310ad9d3c554b)

assessment_items.question_content:
Andrey starts working on his science project at $4{:}22\text{ p.m.}$ He finishes $48$ minutes later. 

**Which number line below graphs when Andrey finishes working on his science project?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $4{:}22$ when Andrey starts working on his science project.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each tick mark on the number line represents $1$ minute.
So, we need to move **right** on the number line $48$ minutes.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{4{:}22} +{} &\greenD{3}\text{ minutes} = 4{:}25\\\\
4{:}25 +{} & \purpleD{35}\text{ minutes} = 5{:}00 \\\\
5{:}00 +{}&\goldE{10}\text{ minutes} = \goldE{5{:}10}\\\\
\end{align}$
$\greenD{3}\text{ minutes}+\purpleD{35}\text{ minutes}+\goldE{10}\text{ minutes}=48\text{ minutes}$
Andrey finishes working on his science project at $5{:}10 \text{ p.m.}$

[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Amira starts raking leaves at $2{:}32\text{ p.m.}$ She finishes $45$ minutes later. 

**Which number line below graphs when Amira finishes raking leaves?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $2{:}32$ when Amira starts raking leaves.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each tick mark on the number line represents $1$ minute.
So, we need to move **right** on the number line $45$ minutes.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{2{:}32} +{} &\greenD{3}\text{ minutes} = 2{:}35\\\\
2{:}35 +{} & \purpleD{40}\text{ minutes} = 3{:}15 \\\\
3{:}15 +{}&\goldE{2}\text{ minutes} = \goldE{3{:}17}\\\\
\end{align}$

$\greenD{3}\text{ minutes}+\purpleD{40}\text{ minutes}+\goldE{2}\text{ minutes}=45\text{ minutes}$
Amira finishes raking leaves at $3{:}17\text{ p.m.}$

[[☃ image 1]]

Mustafa does his chores for $20$ minutes. He finishes his chores at $7{:}15\text{ p.m.}$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $7{:}15$ when Mustafa finishes his chores. 

[[☃ image 1]]

Each tick mark on the number line represents $1$ minute.
So, we need to move **left** on the number line $20$ minutes.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{5}\text{ minutes}+\purpleD{15}\text{ minutes}=20\text{ minutes}$
Mustafa starts his chores at $6{:}55\text{ p.m.}$

[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Rani finishes painting her room at $4{:}17\text{ p.m.}$ She painted for $42$ minutes.

**Which number line below graphs when Rani starts painting?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $4{:}17$ when Rani finishes painting. 

[[☃ image 1]]
Each tick mark on the number line represents $1$ minute.

So, we need to move **left** on the number line $42$ minutes.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{4{:}17} -{} &\greenD{2}\text{ minutes} = 4{:}15\\\\
4{:}15 -{} & \purpleD{15}\text{ minutes} = 4{:}00 \\\\
4{:}00 -{}&\goldE{25}\text{ minutes} = \goldE{3{:}35}\\\\
\end{align}$
$\goldE{25}\text{ minutes}+\purpleD{15}\text{ minutes}+\greenD{2}\text{ minutes}=42\text{ minutes}$

Rani starts painting at $3{:}35\text{ p.m.}$ 

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A number line labeled 3:30 to 4:30 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point is marked at 3:35.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1ff20e892b0abb52ff16d64e86f88a00757c72a5)
![A number line labeled 3:30 to 4:30 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point is marked at 3:45.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eb8cd62f26b09392c3243f219d666ca07eeb1db1)
![A number line labeled 3:30 to 4:30 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point is marked at 3:42.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/291a5ac4bb9b495d37bd599017b873e591fc40d4)

assessment_items.question_content:
Blanca  finishes riding her bike at  $11{:}34\text{ a.m.}$ She rides her bike for $40$ minutes.

**Which number line below shows when Blanca starts her bike ride?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $11{:}34$ when Blanca finishes her bike ride. 

[[☃ image 1]]
Each tick mark on the number line represents $1$ minute.

So, we need to move **left** on the number line $40$ minutes.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{11{:}34} -{} &\greenD{4}\text{ minutes} = 11{:}30\\\\
11{:}30 -{} & \purpleD{30}\text{ minutes} = 11{:}00 \\\\
11{:}00 -{}&\goldE{6}\text{ minutes} = \goldE{10{:}54}\\\\
\end{align}$
$\goldE{6}\text{ minutes}+\purpleD{30}\text{ minutes}+\greenD{4}\text{ minutes}=40\text{ minutes}$

Blanca starts her bike ride at $10{:}54\text{ a.m.}$

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A number line labeled 10:45 to 11:45 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point is marked at 10:49.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1798d4b49aac3e17dd98d72d8db788c95ae409fc)
![A number line labeled 10:45 to 11:45 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point is marked at 11:40.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a51df93ce2c85e564e73587af9f89858d316f8d2)
![A number line labeled 10:45 to 11:45 tick marks  every 1 unit. A point is marked at 10:54.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/71cd0d1dcba6deffc5e0fd9ae4c1785230d2c29c)

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when cookies $\greenD{\text{start}}$ and $\redD{\text{finish}}$ baking.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How long did the cookies bake?**  

[[☃ input-number 5]] minutes
hints:
Each tick mark represents one minute.

The cookies started baking at $\greenD{7{:}17}$ and finished at $\redD{7{:}33}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes it took the cookies to bake, we can find the distance from $\greenD{7{:}17}$ to $\redD{7{:}33}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD3\text{ minutes}+\goldE{10}\text{ minutes}+\blueD{3}\text{ minutes}=16\text{ minutes}$
The cookies baked for $16$ minutes.

options.alt: A number line labeled 7:15 to 7:45 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point labeled start appears 2 tick marks after 7:15, and a tick mark labeled finish appears 3 tick marks after 7:30.

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when Vlad $\greenD{\text{starts}}$  and $\redD{\text{finishes}}$ his math homework.

[[☃ image 1]]



**How long did Vlad do math homework?**  

[[☃ input-number 5]] minutes
hints:
Each tick mark represents one minute.

Vlad started his math homework at $\greenD{4{:}17}$ and finished at $\redD{4{:}41}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes it took Vlad to do his math homework, we can find the distance from $\greenD{4{:}17}$ to $\redD{4{:}41}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD3\text{ minutes}+\goldE{20}\text{ minutes}+\blueD{1}\text{ minutes}=24\text{ minutes}$
It took Vlad $24$ minutes to do his math homework.

options.alt: A number line labeled 4:15 to 4:45 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point labeled start appears 2 tick marks after 4:15, and a point labeled finish appears 1 ticks marks after 4:40.

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when Anna $\greenD{\text{starts}}$ and $\redD{\text{finishes}}$ reading a chapter in her book.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How long did Anna read?**  

[[☃ input-number 5]] minutes
hints:
Each tick mark represents one minute.

Anna started reading at $\greenD{8{:}10}$ and finished at $\redD{8{:}24}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes Anna read, we can find the distance from $\greenD{8{:}10}$ to $\redD{8{:}24}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD{10}\text{ minutes}+\goldE{4}\text{ minutes}=14\text{ minutes}$
Anna read for $14$ minutes.

options.alt: A number line labeled 8:00 to 8:30 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point labeled start appears at 8:10, and a point labeled finish appears 4 ticks marks after 8:20.

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when Jules $\greenD{\text{starts}}$ and $\redD{\text{finishes}}$ eating lunch.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How long did it take Jules to eat lunch?**  

[[☃ input-number 5]] minutes
hints:
Each tick mark represents one minute.

Jules started eating lunch at $\greenD{1{:}06}$  and finished at $\redD{1{:}39}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes it took Jules to eat lunch, we can find the distance from $\greenD{1{:}06}$ to $\redD{1{:}39}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\greenD{1{:}06} +{} &\purpleD{4}\text{ minutes} = 1{:}10\\\\
1{:}10 +{} & \goldE{20}\text{ minutes} = 1{:}30 \\\\
1{:}30 +{}&\blueD{9}\text{ minutes} = \redD{1{:}39}\\\\
\end{align}$
$\purpleD4\text{ minutes}+\goldE{20}\text{ minutes}+\blueD{9}\text{ minutes}=33\text{ minutes}$
It took Jules $33$ minutes to eat lunch.

options.alt: A number line labeled 1 o'clock to 1:45 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point labeled start appears 6 tick marks after 1 o'clock, and a point labeled finish appears 9 ticks marks after 1:30.

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when Mahmoud $\greenD{\text{starts}}$ and $\redD{\text{finishes}}$ playing soccer.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How long did Mahmoud play soccer?** 
 
[[☃ input-number 5]] minutes
hints:
Each tick mark represents one minute.

Mahmoud started playing soccer at $\greenD{9{:}18}$ and finished at $\redD{9{:}57}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes Mahmoud played soccer, we can find the distance from $\greenD{9{:}18}$ to $\redD{9{:}57}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\greenD{9{:}18} +{} &\purpleD{2}\text{ minutes} = 9{:}20\\\\
9{:}20 +{} & \goldE{30}\text{ minutes} = 9{:}50 \\\\
9{:}50 +{}&\blueD{7}\text{ minutes} = \redD{9{:}57}\\\\
\end{align}$
$\purpleD2\text{ minutes}+\goldE{30}\text{ minutes}+\blueD{7}\text{ minutes}=39\text{ minutes}$
Mahmoud played soccer for $39$ minutes.

options.alt: A number line labeled 9:15 to 10:00  with tick marks every 1 unit. A point labeled start appears at 3 tick marks after 9:15, and a point labeled finish appears 3 ticks marks before 10:00.

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when Mahmoud $\greenD{\text{starts}}$ and $\redD{\text{finishes}}$ playing soccer.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How long did Mahmoud play soccer?** 
 
[[☃ input-number 5]] minutes
hints:
Each tick mark represents one minute.

Mahmoud started playing soccer at $\greenD{9{:}32}$ and finished at $\redD{10{:}07}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes Mahmoud played soccer, we can find the distance from $\greenD{9{:}32}$ to $\redD{10{:}07}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\greenD{9{:}32} +{} &\purpleD{3}\text{ minutes} = 9{:}35\\\\
9{:}35 +{} & \goldE{30}\text{ minutes} = 10{:}05 \\\\
10{:}05 +{}&\blueD{2}\text{ minutes} = \redD{10{:}07}\\\\
\end{align}$
$\purpleD3\text{ minutes}+\goldE{30}\text{ minutes}+\blueD{2}\text{ minutes}=35\text{ minutes}$
Mahmoud played soccer for $35$ minutes.

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when Kiran $\greenD{\text{starts}}$ and $\redD{\text{finishes}}$ playing piano.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How long did Kiran play piano?**  

[[☃ input-number 5]] minutes
hints:
Each tick mark represents one minute.

Kiran started playing piano at $\greenD{5{:}56}$  and finished at $\red{6{:}24}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes Kiran played piano, we can find the distance from $\greenD{5{:}56}$ to $\redD{6{:}24}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\greenD{5{:}56} +{} &\purpleD{4}\text{ minutes} = 6{:}00\\\\
6{:}00 +{} & \goldE{20}\text{ minutes} = 6{:}20 \\\\
6{:}20 +{}&\blueD{4}\text{ minutes} = \redD{6{:}24}\\\\
\end{align}$
$\purpleD4\text{ minutes}+\goldE{20}\text{ minutes}+\blueD{4}\text{ minutes}=28\text{ minutes}$
Kiran played piano for $28$ minutes.

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when Dina $\greenD{\text{starts}}$ and $\redD{\text{finishes}}$ washing her car.

**How long did Dina wash her car?**  

[[☃ input-number 5]] minutes
hints:
Each tick mark represents one minute.

Dina started washing her car at $\greenD{3{:}37}$  and finished at $\redD{4{:}10}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes Dina washed her car, we can find the distance from $\greenD{3{:}37}$ to $\redD{4{:}10}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\greenD{3{:}37} +{} &\purpleD{3}\text{ minutes} = 3{:}40\\\\
3{:}40 +{} & \goldE{20}\text{ minutes} = 4{:}00 \\\\
4{:}00 +{}&\blueD{10}\text{ minutes} = \redD{4{:}10}\\\\
\end{align}$
$\purpleD3\text{ minutes}+\goldE{20}\text{ minutes}+\blueD{10}\text{ minutes}=33\text{ minutes}$
Dina washed her car for $33$ minutes.

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when an Olympic speed skater $\greenD{\text{starts}}$ and $\redD{\text{finishes}}$ her race.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How long did the speed skater race?**  

[[☃ input-number 5]] minutes
hints:
Each tick mark represents one minute.

The speed skater started her race at $\greenD{11{:}52}$ and finished at $\redD{12{:}05}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes it took the speed skater to finish the race, we can find the distance from $\greenD{11{:}52}$ to $\redD{12{:}05}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD3\text{ minutes}+\goldE{10}\text{ minutes}=13\text{ minutes}$
It took the speed skater $13$ minutes to finish the race.

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when Ahmet $\greenD{\text{starts}}$ and $\redD{\text{finishes}}$ walking to his neighbor's house.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How long did Ahmet walk?**  

[[☃ input-number 5]] minutes
hints:
Each tick mark represents one minute.

Ahmet started walking at $\greenD{1{:}47}$ and finished at $\redD{2{:}12}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes Ahmet walked, we can find the distance from $\greenD{1{:}47}$ to $\redD{2{:}12}$.  

[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD3\text{ minutes}+\goldE{20}\text{ minutes}+\blueD{2}\text{ minutes}=25\text{ minutes}$
Ahmet walked for $25$ minutes.

options.alt: A number line labeled 1:45 to 2:15 with tick marks every 1 unit. A point labeled start appears 2 tick marks after 1:45, and a point labeled finish appears at 2 tick marks after 2:10.
